commit.message:
feat: add Top 1000 Most Valuable Cards page as landing page

- New /api/catalog/top endpoint with heuristic valuation engine
  - Price guide data used when available (20 cards with market data)
  - Heuristic scoring for all 10K+ cards based on year, rookie status,
    short print rarity, notes keywords, and set importance
  - Filterable by era, player name, and rookie-only
  - Paginated results with total count

- New /top-cards frontend page with:
  - Hero section with live stats (page value, rookie count, guide count)
  - Era filter pills (Pre-War through Ultra-Modern)
  - Player search with form submission
  - Ranked table with medals for top 3, era badges, RC/SP tags
  - Paginated navigation (50 cards per page)
  - Responsive design with skeleton loading states

- Updated navigation: Top Cards tab added as first nav item
- Root / now redirects to /top-cards as the main landing page
- All 118 tests pass, all 13 frontend pages build clean

commit.message:
feat: Enhance scene diagnostics and assertion capabilities (#31)

* feat: Enhance scene diagnostics and assertion capabilities

- Introduced SceneDiagnostics struct to track entity counts and lifecycle events within scenes.
- Added new fields for tracking total entities, mesh, sprite, text, and physics entity counts, as well as lifecycle counters (created, removed, enter, exit, pause, resume).
- Updated Scene class to maintain diagnostics data during entity addition and removal.
- Enhanced ScriptCommand to support new assertion fields for scene diagnostics, including entity counts and lifecycle events.
- Updated InputScript parsing to handle new assertion types for scenes and global scene counts.
- Implemented variable reference support in assertions for dynamic value comparisons.
- Added comprehensive smoke tests to validate new diagnostics features and assertion functionality.
- Updated existing tests to incorporate new diagnostics checks and ensure backward compatibility.

* fix: correct isFocused diagnostics and unknown-field assertion handling

commit.message:
fix: use botas-fastapi for BotApp in echo-bot-no-mention sample (#59)

* Consolidate TeamsActivity-related types into single file per language

Merge TeamsActivityBuilder, TeamsChannelData, TeamsConversation, and related
types (TenantInfo, ChannelInfo, TeamInfo, MeetingInfo, NotificationInfo) into
the main TeamsActivity file in each language:

- .NET: TeamsActivity.cs now contains all Teams types
- Node: teams-activity.ts now contains all Teams types
- Python: teams_activity.py now contains all Teams types

Deleted redundant files:
- dotnet: TeamsActivityBuilder.cs, TeamsChannelData.cs, TeamsConversation.cs
- node: teams-activity-builder.ts, teams-channel-data.ts, teams-conversation.ts
- python: teams_activity_builder.py, teams_channel_data.py, teams_conversation.py

All tests pass across all three languages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add suggested specification docs

Create the six documentation files listed in the README as suggested
additional docs:

- specs/ActivityPayloads.md — Annotated JSON examples for each activity type
- specs/Configuration.md — Per-language configuration reference
- specs/Middleware.md — Middleware spec with patterns and cross-language ref
- specs/ProactiveMessaging.md — Proactive messaging via ConversationClient
- specs/Samples.md — Walkthrough of all samples with annotated code
- specs/Contributing.md — Behavioral invariants, CI, and new language porting

Update README.md to link to the new docs (replacing the suggested list)
and update specs/README.md to include the new docs in the spec table.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use botas-fastapi for BotApp in echo-bot-no-mention sample

The echo-bot-no-mention sample imported BotApp from botas (core), but
BotApp lives in botas_fastapi. Fixed import and added missing pyproject.toml
with botas-fastapi dependency.

commit.message:
feat(task-state): ADR-038 amendment — bounded cold-start scan + header projection cache (#374)

* feat(task-state): ADR-038 amendment - bounded cold-start scan + metadata projection cache

- Add TaskMetadataHeader/SessionTaskSummary projection (meta_projection.rs)
- Add process-global LRU metadata cache with tiebreak eviction (meta_index.rs, 512 entries)
- Add TaskMetadataRef lazy-load handle, cfg(test)-gated (meta_ref.rs)
- Add StartupBudget with VEX_MAX_STARTUP_TASK_SCANS=200 (config/startup.rs)
- Replace O(N) live_session_task_counts_from with cached metadata scan
- Replace O(N) find_session_task_in_saved_states with metadata-first scan
- Add state_files_from_with_limit() bounded variant
- Delete stale TaskStateLiveSummary / load_live_summary private pair
- Add ADR-038-amendment-2026-04-13.md

Corrects three ADR-038 Extension spec errors: serde early-exit claim,
missing TaskMetadataRef.state field, assert_durable_access bypass.
Uses file.dir (not state_dir_from) for path construction so legacy
search dirs resolve correctly.

* fix: add filesystem-safety validation for agent and team names

Agent and team names are used to derive worktree paths (ADR-034) but
were not validated for path-safety. The existing check enforced
non-empty, length, and uniqueness, yet the doc-comment claimed
filesystem-safe without rejecting path separators, null bytes, control
characters, or the reserved dot names '.' and '..'.

Add is_filesystem_safe() and call it from both validate_agent_names()
and validate_team_members(). Add unit tests covering path separators,
backslashes, control characters, dot/dot-dot names, and a team-name
variant. Existing tests are unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden api module against integer overflow from untrusted input

Audit of src/api/ found four integer-overflow paths reachable from
untrusted server data:

- stream.rs process(): buffer-length check used plain addition that
  could wrap on usize overflow; switched to saturating_add.
- stream.rs apply_chat_compat_tool_delta(): tool_call.index arrives
  from deserialized JSON with no upper bound; clamped to 1024 and
  used saturating_add for block_index.
- stream.rs ensure_chat_compat_tool_state(): resize target used
  index + 1 which could wrap to zero, truncating the vector; switched
  to saturating_add.
- client/mod.rs resolve_max_tokens(): float cast (n as f64 * 0.75)
  as u32 lost precision for large context sizes; replaced with exact
  integer arithmetic (n as u64 * 3) / 4.

No unsafe blocks, raw pointers, FFI boundaries, transmute, or
function-pointer casts exist in the api module. The remaining audit
categories (bad character handling, null-terminator assumptions,
stack allocation sizing, control-flow hardening) are clean by design.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden resolve_max_tokens and chat_compat_messages against edge-case panics

resolve_max_tokens: guard clamp(128, ceiling) against panic when a local
server reports n_ctx < 171 (ceiling < 128 violates clamp min <= max
invariant). Return the reduced ceiling directly in that case.

chat_compat_messages: replace unchecked messages.len() + 1 with
saturating_add to prevent theoretical overflow on extreme input sizes.

Add two regression tests covering n_ctx=100 and n_ctx=1 boundary cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: close adr-038 startup memory gaps and fold review follow-ups

* docs: repair raw url map coverage after performance doc addition

* rename: remove 'meta' brand word from codebase

- Rename task_state files: meta_projection -> task_header,
  meta_index -> header_cache, meta_ref -> lazy_task_handle
- Rename types: TaskMetadataHeader -> TaskStateHeader,
  TaskMetadataRef -> LazyTaskHandle, MetadataCache -> HeaderLruCache,
  TaskMeta -> TaskInfo
- Rename field: TaskDocument.meta -> TaskDocument.info
- Rename functions: cached_metadata -> cached_task_header,
  global_metadata_cache -> global_header_cache,
  reset_metadata_cache_for_tests -> reset_header_cache_for_tests
- Add 'meta' to check_forbidden_names.sh brand_words array
- Update all ADRs, TASKS, and doc references
- Fix Copilot review: add error logging in cached_task_header
- Fix Copilot review: update lazy_task_handle module doc
- Fix stale REPO-RAW-URL-MAP row numbering

cargo check, clippy -D warnings, 1356 nextest tests: all pass
check_forbidden_names.sh: clean

* docs: align ADR wording and follow-log guidance

- replace remaining active-branch task metadata wording with task-state header terminology
- align operator guidance around log follow commands
- rename safe extract/tail wording in active code comments, docs, tests, and local variables
- keep Rust trait syntax and upstream API identifiers unchanged where required

cargo check
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo nextest run
bash scripts/check_forbidden_names.sh

* fix: resolve rustfmt violations in mod.rs and history.rs

* Update cross-reference note in ADR-038

* Update performance.md

* fix: stream bounded startup scan, add fingerprint diagnostics, symmetric team name length

* docs: align ADR-038 follow-up wording

* fix(memory-safety): hard cutover to bounded-only task-state discovery

Remove the unbounded collect-then-truncate path from
state_files_from_with_limit(). All call sites — startup, facade, CLI —
now stream directory entries through the bounded top-k selector.

- state_files_from() routes through the bounded path with
  StartupBudget::default().max_scans as the cap
- state_files_from_with_limit(None) applies the same budget default
  instead of collecting all entries into an unbounded Vec
- Remove unused state_files_in_dir() private helper (legacy path)
- Pre-allocate Vec::with_capacity in all facade list operations
- Update ADR-038 amendment and performance.md to reflect the hard cutover
- Rename compat test to match new bounded-always semantics

Validated: cargo check, clippy, fmt, nextest (1357 passed),
check_forbidden_names (clean), check-disk-policy (13 passed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
website: add @docusaurus/faster to fix build failure with future.v4 enabled (#451)

Docusaurus 3.10.0 requires `@docusaurus/faster` to be explicitly
installed when `future: { v4: true }` is set in `docusaurus.config.ts`,
but the package was missing from dependencies, causing `docusaurus
build` to fail with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.

## Changes

- **`website/package.json`**: add `@docusaurus/faster: 3.10.0` under
`dependencies`
- **`website/package-lock.json`**: updated lockfile to reflect the new
dependency

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add PM agent with brainstorming process

- agents/pm.md defines the full brainstorming + requirements flow
- 9-step process: context → questions → approaches → design → test strategy → spec → review
- Testability is the tiebreaker between approaches
- Large requests get decomposed into sub-projects first
- Wired into copilot-instructions.md and pipeline.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor: Establish package definitions as single source of truth (#8)

* Refactor: Establish package definitions as single source of truth

Implements Package first-class citizen architecture with the following changes:

1. Created modules/package-definitions/ structure:
   - Centralized package definitions with metadata, settings, and backend references
   - Sample implementations for git, hello, and hyprland packages
   - Each definition contains: packageId, metadata, defaultSettings, backends

2. Refactored catalog system to derive from definitions:
   - modules/packages/catalog/home.nix now merges definitions with legacy entries
   - modules/packages/catalog/system.nix now merges definitions with legacy entries
   - Definitions take precedence over legacy hardcoded entries
   - Allows gradual migration to definition system

3. Refactored projection registry to derive from definitions:
   - modules/projection/backends/home-manager/packages/default.nix loads definitions
   - Backend implementation paths derived from definition references
   - Maintains backward compatibility with legacy registry entries

4. Added comprehensive tests:
   - tests/package-definitions.nix validates definition structure and derivation
   - Tests verify metadata, settings, backend paths, and catalog integration

5. Updated documentation:
   - docs/package-metadata.md now explains single source of truth architecture
   - Documented package definition structure and workflow

This maintains all existing boundaries (User/Host/Relation model, no top-level
profile.packages, no new PackageBinding objects) while enabling single-point
package management for future additions.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Yttehs-HDX <72240633+Yttehs-HDX@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: Phase 1 package definitions - corrections for mergeable PR

Addresses feedback to create a focused Phase 1 implementation:

1. Fixed hello package inconsistency:
   - hello uses crossPlatformUserPackage (home-only)
   - Tests now correctly verify it's NOT in system catalog
   - Removed incorrect assumption it should be cross-scope

2. Implemented auto-discovery for package definitions:
   - Loader now uses builtins.readDir to auto-discover subdirectories
   - Adding new package requires only creating the directory + default.nix
   - No manual registration needed in loader

3. Removed defaultSettings from definitions:
   - Not yet wired into normalize/context/projectionInputs pipeline
   - Removed from git, hello, hyprland definitions
   - Updated tests to not assert on defaultSettings
   - Cleaner Phase 1 scope

4. Added behavioral tests:
   - Test registry derivation from definitions
   - Verify projectors are callable functions
   - Goes beyond structural tests to validate integration

5. Updated documentation to match actual completion:
   - Changed to \"Phase 1\" framing throughout
   - Explicitly listed unimplemented capabilities
   - Removed claims about defaultSettings runtime integration
   - Added note about auto-discovery mechanism

No semantic changes to package metadata or existing catalog entries.
This is a focused, mergeable Phase 1 implementation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Yttehs-HDX <72240633+Yttehs-HDX@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: Phase 1 package definitions - corrections for mergeable PR

Addresses all blockers for Phase 1 merge:

1. Fixed projection registry import path:
   - Changed from ../../../package-definitions to ../../../../package-definitions
   - Corrects path from modules/projection/backends/home-manager/packages/

2. Fixed hello package to support both home and system scopes:
   - hello is used in BOTH user.packages AND host.packages in tests
   - Changed from crossPlatformUserPackage to explicit cross-scope metadata
   - Now supports all targets (home + system) as host-owned package
   - Tests verify hello appears in both home and system catalogs
   - Added assertHelloCrossScope test to validate dual-scope semantics

3. Reverted pipewire semantic changes:
   - owner: host (was incorrectly changed to user)
   - requiresDesktop: false (was incorrectly changed to true)
   - Restored original unsupportedReason/unsupportedSuggestion text
   - No unrelated package semantics modified in this PR

4. Documented lib=builtins limitation:
   - Explicitly noted in Phase 1 limitations
   - Definitions should not depend on lib helpers currently
   - Will be improved in future phase

5. Updated documentation to accurate Phase 1 scope:
   - Listed three migrated packages: git, hyprland, hello
   - Clarified hello as cross-scope example
   - Documented lib limitation
   - Clear separation of implemented vs future capabilities

6. Tests now validate real semantics:
   - assertHelloCrossScope validates dual-scope support
   - assertSystemCatalogInclusions correctly expects hello
   - assertHomeCatalogDerivation validates all three packages
   - All tests reflect actual repository usage patterns

Phase 1 Complete Scope:
✅ Package definition engineering single source of truth
✅ Auto-discovery loader (builtins.readDir)
✅ Catalog derivation from definitions
✅ Projection registry derivation
✅ Three sample migrations with different patterns
✅ Behavioral + structural tests
✅ Hybrid system (definitions + legacy coexist)

Phase 1 Limitations (for future phases):
❌ lib helper support in definitions
❌ defaultSettings runtime integration
❌ Complete package migration
❌ Full definition validation/error reporting

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Yttehs-HDX <72240633+Yttehs-HDX@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: Phase 1 package definitions - final corrections for merge

Addresses all remaining blockers for Phase 1 merge:

1. Fixed projection registry import path:
   - Changed from ../../../package-definitions to ../../../../package-definitions
   - Corrects path from modules/projection/backends/home-manager/packages/

2. Fixed hello package to support both home and system scopes:
   - hello is used in BOTH user.packages AND host.packages in tests
   - Changed to explicit cross-scope metadata with owner=host
   - Supports all targets (home + system) as host-owned package
   - Tests verify hello appears in both home and system catalogs
   - Added assertHelloCrossScope test to validate dual-scope semantics
   - Note: No user-side bindings exist in repository (only host-side)

3. Reverted pipewire semantic changes:
   - owner: host (was incorrectly changed to user)
   - requiresDesktop: false (was incorrectly changed to true)
   - Restored original unsupportedReason/unsupportedSuggestion text
   - No unrelated package semantics modified in this PR

4. Documented lib=builtins limitation:
   - Explicitly noted in Phase 1 limitations
   - Definitions should not depend on lib helpers currently
   - Will be improved in future phase

5. Updated documentation to accurate Phase 1 scope:
   - Listed three migrated packages: git, hyprland, hello
   - Clarified hello as cross-scope host-owned example
   - Documented lib limitation and hybrid mode
   - Corrected catalog/registry descriptions to match hybrid implementation
   - Fixed warning behavior description (triggers when not in ANY catalog)
   - Clear separation of implemented vs future capabilities

6. Tests now validate real semantics:
   - assertHelloCrossScope validates dual-scope support
   - assertSystemCatalogInclusions correctly expects hello
   - assertHomeCatalogDerivation validates all three packages
   - All tests reflect actual repository usage patterns

Phase 1 Complete Scope:
✅ Package definition engineering single source of truth
✅ Auto-discovery loader (builtins.readDir)
✅ Hybrid catalog (definitions + legacy coexist, definitions override)
✅ Hybrid projection registry (definitions + legacy coexist)
✅ Three sample migrations: git (user/home), hyprland (desktop), hello (cross-scope/host)
✅ Behavioral + structural tests
✅ Documentation accurate to implementation

Phase 1 Limitations (for future phases):
❌ lib helper support in definitions (currently lib=builtins)
❌ defaultSettings field and runtime integration
❌ Complete package migration (hybrid mode with legacy entries)
❌ Full definition validation/error reporting

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

* refactor: package definitions for better modularity and maintainability

* docs: refactor package metadata to be definition-driven (Phase 1)

commit.message:
fix: CAMPAIGN_SHEET_ID empty string now falls back to default sheet ID

os.environ.get() with a default only works when var is UNSET.
When bot.env contains 'CAMPAIGN_SHEET_ID=' (empty value), Python returns
empty string, causing URL 'spreadsheets/d//edit#gid=0'. Fixed with 'or'.
Also documented CAMPAIGN_SHEET_ID in bot.env.template.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: release v1.0.9

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(jetson): cv2.imwrite return check + chdir error handling

T2-01: _save_prediction_image now checks cv2.imwrite return value.
On failure, cleans up partial file and returns None. Caller in
predict() guards os.unlink with 'if image_path' to prevent
TypeError on None.

T2-03: main.py os.chdir() wrapped in try/except. On failure,
prints clear fatal error to stderr and exits immediately instead
of continuing with broken relative paths.

1. TZ env var

commit.message:
Fix worker spawning: process.execPath is copilot.exe, not node

process.execPath in the Copilot CLI runtime points to copilot.exe (a
standalone PE binary), not a Node.js binary. Spawning workers with it
fails because copilot.exe doesn't understand -e or .mjs arguments.

Added extensions/lib/resolve-node.mjs with resolveNodeBinary() that:
1. Probes process.execPath to see if it actually is Node
2. Falls back to node/node.exe on PATH via where/which
3. Caches the result for subsequent calls
4. Throws a clear error if neither works

Updated both ado-pr-watcher and ado-build-watcher to use it.
Updated CI expected files and copilot instructions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add suggested specification docs (#58)

* Consolidate TeamsActivity-related types into single file per language

Merge TeamsActivityBuilder, TeamsChannelData, TeamsConversation, and related
types (TenantInfo, ChannelInfo, TeamInfo, MeetingInfo, NotificationInfo) into
the main TeamsActivity file in each language:

- .NET: TeamsActivity.cs now contains all Teams types
- Node: teams-activity.ts now contains all Teams types
- Python: teams_activity.py now contains all Teams types

Deleted redundant files:
- dotnet: TeamsActivityBuilder.cs, TeamsChannelData.cs, TeamsConversation.cs
- node: teams-activity-builder.ts, teams-channel-data.ts, teams-conversation.ts
- python: teams_activity_builder.py, teams_channel_data.py, teams_conversation.py

All tests pass across all three languages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add suggested specification docs

Create the six documentation files listed in the README as suggested
additional docs:

- specs/ActivityPayloads.md — Annotated JSON examples for each activity type
- specs/Configuration.md — Per-language configuration reference
- specs/Middleware.md — Middleware spec with patterns and cross-language ref
- specs/ProactiveMessaging.md — Proactive messaging via ConversationClient
- specs/Samples.md — Walkthrough of all samples with annotated code
- specs/Contributing.md — Behavioral invariants, CI, and new language porting

Update README.md to link to the new docs (replacing the suggested list)
and update specs/README.md to include the new docs in the spec table.

commit.message:
feat: Tournament mode — single-elimination bracket with 8/16 players, Firestore sync, champion badge and scoring (#84)

* feat: implement tournament mode single-elimination bracket

- Add domain models: TournamentPlayer, TournamentMatch, TournamentStatus, Tournament
- Add TournamentRepository interface
- Add CreateTournamentUseCase and AdvanceTournamentUseCase
- Add FirestoreTournamentRepository with Firestore sync
- Add TournamentViewModel, TournamentSetupScreen, TournamentBracketScreen
- Add tournament navigation routes and menu button
- Add unit tests for CreateTournamentUseCase and TournamentViewModel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: cancleeric <9006516+cancleeric@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove repositories block from core/build.gradle.kts

FAIL_ON_PROJECT_REPOS is set in settings.gradle.kts dependencyResolutionManagement.
mavenCentral() is already declared there and applies to all modules including :core.
Adding a project-level repositories block causes a build failure.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: cancleeric <9006516+cancleeric@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Hurricane CTO <hurricane-cto@hurricanedigital.com>

commit.message:
Add top 10,000 canonical baseball card database

Comprehensive seed database covering 481 players across 368 set-years
from 17 manufacturers, spanning 1909 T206 to 2025 modern releases.

- Generator script (scripts/generate_top_cards.py) with full player
  database organized by era and importance tier (1-5)
- 151 hand-curated key cards with exact card numbers (T206 Wagner,
  1952 Topps Mantle #311, 1989 UD Griffey #1, etc.)
- 1,758 rookie cards identified and flagged
- Covers all major sets: Topps, Bowman, Fleer, Donruss, Upper Deck,
  Score, Topps Chrome, Bowman Chrome, SP, Finest, Heritage, Prizm
- Decade distribution: 1900s-1940s (299), 1950s-70s (1,406),
  1980s-90s (3,864), 2000s-20s (4,444)
- Seed function skips duplicates (39 iconic cards already existed)
- All 118 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
